questions.question: The chief male deity of the Indus Valley Civilization was-
questions.explanation: The chief male deity of the Indus Valley Civilization was the Pashupati Mahadeva, who is the lord of animals represented in seals as sitting in yogic posture. He is surrounded by four animals (elephant, tiger, buffalo and rhino) and two deer appear at his feet. During this civilization, there was also instances of Lingam worship

questions.question: Which of the following is the only Indus site where the remains of a horse have been found?
questions.explanation: Surkotada is located in Kutchh district of Gujarat and this is the only place where the remains of a horse have been found though it is not confirmed whether Indus people knew to use a horse. Apart from this, oval grave and pot burials have also been found in this place

questions.question: The city of Harappa was located on the banks of which among the following rivers?
questions.explanation: The city of Harappa is one of the most prominent cities of the Indus Valley civilization. It is located on the banks of the river Ravi and at present, it is situated in Larkana district in the Sindh Province of Pakistan. The city was discovered by RD Banerji in 1922

questions.question: Which of the following is considered as the oldest deciphered script in Indian subcontinent?
questions.explanation: The oldest script in Indian subcontinent is Harappan script but the oldest deciphered script is Bramhi script which is known from about 5th century BC. Later Indian scripts developed from Bramhi script

questions.question: "The Aryans were migrated to India from the Central Asia Region," who among the following propounded this theory?
questions.explanation: The location of the original home of the Aryans still remains a controversial point. Some scholars believe that the Aryans were native to India whereas some were of the view that they migrated from outside (Central Asia, according to Max Mueller and Europe/ Arctic Region, according to BG Tilak). The Vedic Civilization was prevalent during 2000BC-1500 BC

questions.question: Which among the following is considered as the oldest and largest Bramhana text in the Vedic literature?
questions.explanation: The Bramhanas explain the hymns of the Vedas. They are written in prose and are ritualistic in nature. 'Bramha' means sacrifice. The various sacrifices and rituals are discussed in these texts. Every Veda has certain Bramhanas attached to it: Aitreya and Sankhayan (Rig Veda), Panchvisa, Chhandogya (Sama Veda), Shatapatha and Taittariya (Yajur Veda) and Gopatha (Atharva)

questions.question: The Gayatri Mantra was dedicated to whom among the following?
questions.explanation: In the Rig-Vedic era, Indra, Agni and Varuna were the most popular deities. Apart from them, Surya was worshipped in five forms: Surya, Savitri, Mitra, Pushan and Vishnu. The famous Gayatri Mantra was dedicated to Savitri who was the God of light

questions.question: Which among the following ancient Indian Kings was also known as Ugrasena?
questions.explanation: Mahapadma was the founder of the Nanada Dynasty in Magadha. He is known as Sarvakshantranak (uprooter of all the Kshatriyas) and Ugrasena (owner of huge army). He is recognized as Ekrat or the sole monarch. He seems to have overthrown all the dynasties which ruled at the time of the Shishunagas. Mahapadma is considered as the first empire builder of Indian history

questions.question: The Macedonian ruler Alexander passed away at the age of 33 years in -
questions.explanation: Alexander succeeded his father Philip as the king of Macedonia only at the age of 20 years. He conquered most of India and defeated Porus in the Battle of Hidaspes in 326 BC. After a brilliant victory at Sakala, the Greek forces reached the Beas but Alexander had to return from this place as his soldiers refused to go further. Alexander marched back in 325 BC but he died in Babylon at the age of 33 in 323 BC

questions.question: Who among the following was the first teacher to Gautama Buddha after he left home for the search of enlightenment?
questions.explanation: Gautama Buddha left home at the age of 29 and this is known as his Mahabhinishkramana. After this, he became a wandering ascetic when his first teacher was Alara Kalama, who was a Sankhya Philosopher. From him, Buddha learned the technique of meditation. His next teacher was Udraka Ramaputra. He achieved his enlightenment at the age of 35 years

questions.question: Milindapanho is related to which among the following kings?
questions.explanation: Milindapdianho is a Buddhist text which is written in Pali. The book is basically based on a dialogue between Milinda (the Indo-Greek ruler Menander) and Buddhist saint Nagasena. It is a great example of the Buddhist ideologies

questions.question: Who among the following is known as the first Tirthamnkara in the Jain tradition?
questions.explanation: According to Jain tradition, there were 24 Tirthankaras (literally Ford maker, across the stream of existence), the first of whom was Rishabha or Adinath and the last was Mahavira. Risabha is also considered as a reincarnation of Vishnu and his name is also found in Rig Veda

questions.question: According to Jainism, how many types of knowledge are there?
questions.explanation: According to the Jain tradition, there are 5 types of knowledge and they are: • Mati jnana: Perception through activation of sense organs including the mind • Shruta Jnana: Knowledge through scriptures • Avadhi Jnana: Clairvoyant perception • Manahprayaya Jnana: Telepathic knowledge • Keval jnana: Temporal knowledge or omniscience

questions.question: The Treaty of Pondicherry was signed in the year-
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Pondicherry or the Treaty of Godehu was signed by the French governor Godehu with the British in the year 1754 in order to put an end to the Second Anglo-French War that started in 1749. The British and the French were involved in conflicts over their territories in India and there were three wars fought between them to decide the supremacy. At last, the French were confined to certain places in India such as Pondicherry

questions.question: Who transferred the capital of Bengal from Dacca to Murshidabad?
questions.explanation: In 1717, Murshid Quli Khan was appointed as the governor of Bengal by the Mughal Emperor Farrukh Siyar. He was also granted the Governor ship of Orissa by Farrukh Siyar in 1719. He transferred the capital of Bengal from Dacca to Murshidabad

questions.question: The first French factory in India was established in which among the following places?
questions.explanation: The French East India Company was formed in the year 1664 under state patronage by Colbert. The first factory of the French was established in 1668 by Francois Caron in Surat in Gujarat and after that another was established at Mausalipatnam in 1669

questions.question: Under the reign of which among the following Governor-generals, Tipu Sultan was finally defeated?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan was the king of Mysore after the death of his father Hyder Ali. He fought valiantly against the British in the Second Anglo Mysore War (1780-84) which ended with the signing of Treaty of Mangalore (1784). The Third Anglo-Mysore War followed in 1790 which ended with Treaty of Seringapatnam in 1792. In the Fourth Anglo-Mysore War that started in 1799 under the reign of Lord Wellesley, Tipu died

questions.question: Maharaja Ranjit Singh was born in the year-
questions.explanation: Maharaja Ranjit Singh was the greatest ruler of his time and was the founder of the Sikh rule in Punjab. Born in 1780 in Gujranwala, he occupied Lahore in 1799 and made it his capital. He conquered Amritsar, Ludhiana, Kangra, Attock, Multan, Kashmir, Hazara, Bannu, Derajat etc. He passed away in 1839 and after his death, the Sikh power was broken by the British

questions.question: Who planned the Permanent Settlement system of revenue collection in India?
questions.explanation: The Permanent Settlement System was introduced by Lord Cornwallis in the year 1793 in Bengal, Bihar, Orissa and districts of Benaras and northern districts. John Shore planned it and under the system, zamindars were declared the owners of their land. Hence, they were allowed to keep 1/11th of the revenue collected while the British received the rest on a fixed basis

questions.question: Bijolia Movement took place in which among the following places?
questions.explanation: Some of the prominent peasant movements in India and their origins: • Indigo Revolt: Bengal (1859) • Pabna Revolt: Bengal (1870) • Deccan Riots: Maharashtra (1875) • Ramosi Movement: Maharashtra (1879) • Champaran: Bihar (1917) • Kheda: Gujarat (1918) • Moplah: Kerala (1921) • Tebhaga: Bengal (1946)

questions.question: Tiruth Singh is related to which among the following tribal revolts during the British Rule?
questions.explanation: Some of the tribal revolts in India and their leaders: • Chuar: Raja Jagannath • Bhills: Sewaram • Ramosi: Chittur Singh, Pratap Singh, Dattaraya Patkar • Ahom: Gomadhar Kunwar • Kol: Buddhu Bhagat • Santhals: Sidhu and Kanhu • Naikda: Rup Singh • Munda: Birsa Munda • Rampas: Alluri Sitaram Raju

questions.question: Dharma Sabha was founded in Calcutta by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: Some socio-religious movements in the 19th-20th centuries and the founders: • Atmiya Sabha: Rammohan Roy • Bramho Samaj: Rammohan Roy • Dharma Sabha: Radhakant Dev • Tattavodhini Sabha: Devendranath Tagore • Paramhansa Mandli: Dadoda Pandurang • Radha Swami Satsang: Tulsi Ram • Bramho Samaj of India: Keshab Chandra Sen • Arya Samaj: Swami Dayanand Saraswati • Indian National Conference: MG Ranade • Theosophical Society: Madam HP Blavatasky and Col. HS Olcott • Sadharan Bramho Samaj: Anand Mohan Bose • Deva Samaj: Shivnarayan Agnihotri

questions.question: Which of the following is considered as the immediate cause of the Revolt of 1857?
options: Nana Sahib was refused pension by the British
Avadh was annexed to the British Empire under the Doctrine of Lapse
questions.explanation: The Revolt of 1857 took place because of a number of reasons and they are: • Political Causes such as Nana Shahib was refused pension, Avadh was annxed in 1856 on charges of mal-administration, Satara, Jhansi, Nagpur were annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse • Economic causes such as heavy taxes, forcibly evictions, discriminatory tariff policy against Indian traders • Socio-religious causes such as abolition of Sati in 1829, legalization of widow remarriage in 1856 • Military cause such as discrimination with Indian soldiers • Immediate cause such as the introduction of Enfield Rifles whose cartridges were believed to have been made of beef and pork both

questions.question: Who authored the book "The First Indian War of Independence 1857-59"?
questions.explanation: Some of the important books on the Revolt of 1857: • Causes of Indian Revolt: Sayed Ahmed Khan • The India War of Independence: VD Savarkar • The Sepoy Mutiny and the Rebellion of 1857: RC Mazumdar • Civil Rebellion in Indian Mutinies: SB Chowdhury • Rebellion, 1857: A Symposium: PC Joshi • 1857: SN Sen

questions.question: Who was the President of Indian National Congress at its first session in 1885?
questions.explanation: The First session of the Indian National Congress was held at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay under the Presidentship of WC Banerji, a veteran lawyer of Calcutta. 72 delegates attended the session from all over India. The Indian National Congress was initially named as the Indian National Union under which name it was first called with the initiative of AO Hume, a retired civil servant and an Englishman

questions.question: Who said the following regarding the Indian National Congress? "INC is a begging institute."
questions.explanation: Some of the select opinions regarding the Indian National Congress: • INC represents only a microscopic minorities: Lord Dufferin • The Congress is tottering to its fall, and one of my great ambitions, while in India, is to assist it to a peaceful demise: Lord Curzon • INC should distinguish between begging and claiming the rights: BG Tilak • INC playing with bubbles: Bipin Chandra Pal

questions.question: Who said that "Swaraj is my birth right and I will have it"?
questions.explanation: BG Tilak was one of the extremist leaders during the initial years of INC and he was popularly known as 'Lokmanya'. He launched two newspapaers 'Kesari' (Marathi) and 'Maratha' (English). He organized Ganpati festival and Shivaji Festival. He was deported to Mandalay jail for writing seditious articles and later started the Home Rule Movement in 1916. One of his most famous books was 'Gita Rahasya'

questions.question: The Partition of Bengal came into effect in 1905 on which of the following dates?
questions.explanation: The Partition of Bengal was one of the most debated topics in the first half of the 20th century in the history of Indian national movement. It came into effect on October 16, 1905 through a royal proclamation, reducing the old province of Bengal by size by creating a new province of East Bengal, which later on became East Pakistan and present day Bangladesh It was actually done to destabilize the centre of political activism which was Bengal at that time and after a lot of protests, it was overturned by the British

questions.question: Who among the following was the founder of the All India Muslim League?
questions.explanation: The All India Muslim League was established in 1906 by Nawab Salimullah in Dacca. The league supported the partition of Bengal and opposed the Swadeshi Movement as well. They demanded special safeguards for the Muslim community and also asked for a special electorate of Muslims. This finally led to communal differences between the Hindus and the Muslims

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was split in which among the following sessions of the INC?
questions.explanation: In the Surat session of the Indian National Congress, the INC got divided into two parts and they are the extremists and the moderates. The extremists were led by BG Tilak, Lala Lajpat Rai and Bipin Chandra Pal and the moderates were led by Gopal Krishna Gokhale. After the fiasco that took place, INC decided to nominate Rashbihari Ghose as the President of the session.
